title: Worker with idempotency key in args + database check
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.WebhookDeliveryWorker` (queue `:webhooks`, max_attempts 10). perform/1 takes `%{"idempotency_key" => key, "url" => url, "body" => body}`. The worker must: (1) check `MyApp.Webhooks.find_delivery(key)` — if a record exists with status `:sent`, return `:ok` immediately without re-sending; (2) if no record or status is `:pending`, POST to the url with `MyApp.HTTP.post(url, body)`; (3) on success, call `MyApp.Webhooks.mark_sent(key)` and return `:ok`; (4) on `{:error, reason}`, return `{:error, reason}` so Oban retries. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/webhook_delivery_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Full idempotency-key pattern with a database short-circuit. Cleanly frames the philosophical difference between 'redo work' and 'ensure work happened exactly once'.
